rationale: The citing paper does more than list the cited work in background; it uses Nunes (2000) to justify key assumptions in its model of multipart pricing. Specifically, Nunes is cited as prior evidence that consumers account for expected consumption when choosing plans and as part of the literature establishing usage uncertainty as important. These roles directly inform the citing paper's conceptual setup for jointly modeling choice and usage.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Nunes (2000) as behavioral grounding for its core assumptions that expected usage and tariff structure are jointly considered under multipart pricing. It uses that evidence to motivate its own conjoint-based structural model of choice and consumption under usage uncertainty.
